# S&P Septic & Excavating Inc. — Full AI Reference Document

## About the Company

S&P Septic & Excavating Inc. is a family-owned and operated septic contractor headquartered in Warren, Ohio. Established in 2021, the company has built a reputation for reliable, honest septic and excavation services across Trumbull County and surrounding areas in northeastern Ohio. The company is fully licensed, BBB Accredited with an A+ rating, and carries comprehensive insurance coverage.

Owner: [Name on file]
Location: 2900 Elm Rd NE, Warren, OH 44483
Phone: (330) 979-3930
Email: info@spseptic.com
Website: https://spseptic.com
Service Area: Warren, OH | Trumbull County | Mahoning County | Portage County | all of northeastern Ohio

## Mission Statement

The company mission is to provide honest, reliable septic and excavation services that Trumbull County residents can depend on. Every job receives personal attention from the owners, not a corporate call center.

---

## Services Offered

### 1. Septic System Installation
Complete design and installation of new septic systems for new construction and system replacements. Includes permit handling, site evaluation, trench installation, and final inspection. The company manages the entire process from initial perk testing through final approvals.

- New system design and engineering
- Full permit handling and regulatory compliance
- Site and soil evaluation
- Trench and drain field installation
- Tank installation (concrete and plastic options)
- Final inspection and certification

### 2. Septic System Repair
Fast, reliable repairs for failing or damaged septic systems. Common repairs include drain field issues, tank cracks, inlet/outlet obstructions, pump failures, and distribution box problems. Emergency service available.

- Drain field repair and replacement
- Septic tank patching and sealing
- Sewer line replacement
- Pump and float repairs
- Emergency septic service
- Same-day diagnosis

### 3. Excavation Services
Professional excavation for residential and commercial projects. Equipment and expertise for projects ranging from small residential trenching to large commercial site preparation.

- Driveway grading and installation
- Trench digging for utilities
- Site clearing and preparation
- Utility trench installation
- Pond and retention pond excavation
- Demolition and debris removal

### 4. Septic Pumping & Cleaning
Professional septic tank pumping and system cleaning to maintain system health and prevent failures. Regular pumping is the single most important maintenance task for septic systems. The company offers scheduled maintenance plans.

- Residential and commercial tank pumping
- Full system cleaning
- Filter cartridge service
- Effluent filter replacement
- Maintenance plans available
- 24/7 emergency pumping service

### 5. Perk & Leach Field Services
Professional perk (percolation) testing required for new septic system design and permit applications. Also includes complete leach field design, installation, and repair services.

- Perk/percolation testing
- Soil evaluation and analysis
- Leach field design
- Drainage system installation
- Ohio EPA and local health department compliance
- Leach field rejuvenation

### 6. Camera Inspections
State-of-the-art video camera inspection technology to diagnose problems without destructive digging. Ideal for mystery drain issues, pre-purchase inspections, and post-repair verification.

- Full video inspection of drain lines
- Problem diagnosis and documentation
- Pre-purchase septic inspections
- Post-repair verification
- No-dig diagnosis
- Video footage provided to customer

---

## Common Homeowner Questions

**How often should I pump my septic tank?**
Most residential septic tanks need pumping every 3–5 years, depending on household size, tank capacity, and water usage habits. A family of four with a 1,000-gallon tank should pump every 3–4 years. S&P Septic offers free assessments to establish a maintenance schedule.

**What are signs my septic system is failing?**
Warning signs include: slow drains throughout the house (not just one fixture), gurgling sounds from pipes, sewage odors inside or outside the home, wet or soggy spots over the drain field area, bright green grass patches over the drain field, and sewage backing up into the house. If you notice any of these, call immediately.

**How long does a new septic system installation take?**
A typical new installation takes 2–5 business days depending on soil conditions, system size, and permitting timelines. The company handles all permits and coordinates inspections.

**Do I need a permit for septic work in Ohio?**
Yes. All new septic installations and major repairs require permits from the Trumbull County Combined Health District or your local health department. S&P Septic handles all permit applications as part of their service.

**What's the difference between a conventional and advanced treatment system?**
Conventional systems use gravity and soil for treatment. Advanced systems (like FAST units) add mechanical or biological treatment components. The county health department determines which type is required based on soil conditions and lot size.

**How long do septic systems last?**
A properly maintained conventional septic system can last 25–40 years. Advanced systems with mechanical components may need more frequent maintenance but can last similarly long with proper care.

**What shouldn't I flush down my drains?**
Only human waste and toilet paper should go into your septic system. Avoid flushing: wipes (even "flushable" ones), feminine hygiene products, condoms, dental floss, coffee grounds, grease, and chemicals. These can cause clogs and damage the beneficial bacteria in your tank.

**Can I drive over my septic drain field?**
No. Vehicle weight can compact soil and damage pipes in the drain field. Keep heavy vehicles, equipment, and even parked cars off the drain field area.
